title: I Done 1

jokes.text: Man, you looked just like a cow caught in the headlights.
jokes.setup: Will describes Uncle Phil's surprised expression at the party
jokes.punchline: Uses 'cow' instead of 'deer' - mixing up the idiom while also making a fat joke
jokes.explanation: Classic Will Smith delivery - the malapropism works on two levels, showing his street smart vs book smart gap while also being a subtle dig at Phil's weight

jokes.text: The correct term is 'deer caught in the headlights.' / Oh, yeah, yeah. I know. / Forty-eight, 49, a jillion.
jokes.setup: Carlton corrects Will's malapropism, Will acknowledges the correction
jokes.punchline: Will immediately makes another mistake counting Phil's age, ending with 'a jillion'
jokes.explanation: Good callback structure - Will's 'I know' is immediately contradicted by another mistake, showing he doesn't really know

jokes.text: Mom, how old are you? / Younger than your father. / But everything's younger than Dad.
jokes.setup: Ashley asks Vivian's age, gets a deflective answer
jokes.punchline: Will's observation that everything is younger than Phil
jokes.explanation: Simple age joke with good timing, though fairly predictable

jokes.text: And a nephew that's, uh... That's, uh... Hey, buddy, you're gonna love kindergarten.
jokes.setup: Phil lists everyone's accomplishments, struggles to find something positive about Will
jokes.punchline: Gives up and redirects to Nicky instead
jokes.explanation: Great uncomfortable pause moment - Phil's struggle is palpable and the redirect to the toddler is perfectly timed

jokes.text: Well, it looks like we're all in the fast lane. Except for Will. I mean, someone has to be stuck on the soft shoulder.
jokes.setup: Carlton observes everyone's success
jokes.punchline: Uses highway metaphor to exclude Will, calling him stuck on the soft shoulder
jokes.explanation: Perfectly Carlton - uses sophisticated metaphor to deliver a cutting insult. The 'soft shoulder' terminology is both pretentious and precise

jokes.text: Y'all hear what he said? Stuck on the soft shoulder. Hey, are all the other munchkins as funny as you?
jokes.setup: Will repeats Carlton's insult to draw attention to it
jokes.punchline: Calls Carlton a munchkin, referencing his short stature
jokes.explanation: Standard Will comeback - height joke is predictable but the 'munchkin' reference adds some color

jokes.text: Great, I love champagne. I mean, I love the look of it. The color and the bubbles.
jokes.setup: Will gets excited about champagne
jokes.punchline: Quickly clarifies he only likes looking at it, revealing he can't/won't drink
jokes.explanation: Mild awkwardness as Will tries to save face about not drinking - relatable but not particularly clever

jokes.text: Oh, Geoffrey, I'm gonna miss you. Oh, we have to have a special going-away dinner for you. What do you wanna make?
jokes.setup: Hilary expresses sentiment about missing Geoffrey
jokes.punchline: Asks what HE wants to make for his own going-away dinner
jokes.explanation: Perfectly captures Hilary's oblivious self-centeredness - she's so used to being served she can't conceive of making the dinner herself

jokes.text: Don't worry, Will, you can come to kindergarten with me.
jokes.setup: Family toasts to everyone's new lives, Will is excluded
jokes.punchline: 5-year-old Nicky offers to let Will come to kindergarten
jokes.explanation: Brutal in its innocence - the toddler offering Will his level of schooling perfectly punctuates Will's lack of direction

jokes.text: Geoffrey, get in here, I need you. / Oh, don't you just love it? / Love it.
jokes.setup: Hilary gives Geoffrey a going-away gift - a bell to summon him
jokes.punchline: Geoffrey's deadpan 'Love it' response to the insulting gift
jokes.explanation: Dark irony - giving someone leaving a tool to summon them back, Geoffrey's dry response sells the absurdity

jokes.text: I'm not getting older, I'm just getting better. / That's the same thing I'd say if I was on the downside of the hill.
jokes.setup: Phil uses the cliché about getting better not older
jokes.punchline: Will points out that's exactly what someone over the hill would say
jokes.explanation: Good deconstruction of a tired phrase - Will's logic is sound and cuts through Phil's denial

jokes.text: Before I go to Princeton, there's a few loose ends I'd like to tie up. I've taken the liberty of re-drafting your will. Now, if you'll sign right here and initial this little box... in the event you lapse into a coma, that'll give us the right to pull the plug.
jokes.setup: Carlton mentions tying up loose ends before college
jokes.punchline: Reveals he's rewritten Phil's will and wants power to pull the plug
jokes.explanation: Brilliantly dark and perfectly Carlton - his matter-of-fact delivery of something so morbid, presented as practical planning, is comedy gold

jokes.text: How about a little sensitivity? / All right, but if he keels over today, we're screwed.
jokes.setup: Will calls for sensitivity about Phil's mortality
jokes.punchline: Carlton agrees but immediately says if Phil dies today they're screwed
jokes.explanation: Perfect follow-up to the will joke - Carlton's 'sensitivity' is purely practical, not emotional

jokes.text: Happy birthday, Mr. Banks. Heard you had quite a soiree last night. That's French for 'shindig.'
jokes.setup: Jazz uses the fancy word 'soiree'
jokes.punchline: Immediately translates it to the much more casual 'shindig'
jokes.explanation: Typical Jazz - tries to sound sophisticated but the translation undermines it, showing he's trying too hard

jokes.text: What's French for 'unwelcomed guest'?
jokes.setup: Jazz's French lesson moment
jokes.punchline: Phil asks for the French translation of 'unwelcomed guest' - clearly referring to Jazz
jokes.explanation: Perfect comeback - uses Jazz's own setup against him while making his unwelcome status crystal clear

jokes.text: They're false teeth. They were my grandmom's. She don't need them no more.
jokes.setup: Jazz gives Phil a birthday gift
jokes.punchline: It's his dead grandmother's dentures
jokes.explanation: Deeply inappropriate gift that's both morbid and gross - classic Jazz thoughtlessness with dark implications

jokes.text: Man, Uncle Phil, you beat your own record by, like, 10 feet.
jokes.setup: Phil throws Jazz out
jokes.punchline: Will measures the distance as a personal best
jokes.explanation: Good callback to the running gag - treating Jazz's ejection like a sport with measurable improvement

jokes.text: There he is. The oldest man in the world.
jokes.setup: Phil enters the room after the Jazz incident
jokes.punchline: Will greets him as 'the oldest man in the world'
jokes.explanation: Simple age joke - direct but lacks creativity, just pushing the 50th birthday theme

jokes.text: But that won't get my dry cleaning picked up. Chop-chop, Geoffrey.
jokes.setup: Geoffrey says he and Carlton will write when he goes to London
jokes.punchline: Carlton dismisses the sentiment and orders Geoffrey to work
jokes.explanation: Perfect Carlton entitlement - reduces emotional connection to practical needs and immediately orders service

jokes.text: And to think I fished that boy out of the pool.
jokes.setup: Carlton's entitled behavior toward Geoffrey
jokes.punchline: Geoffrey reveals he once saved Carlton's life and now regrets it
jokes.explanation: Dark humor gold - Geoffrey's dry regret about saving Carlton's life is both shocking and perfectly earned given Carlton's behavior

jokes.text: I don't think so. We agreed I could pick out the colors. / We agreed that you would have input. / That's right, and I'm inputting these colors into our apartment. / My apartment.
jokes.setup: Sisters arguing about apartment decoration
jokes.punchline: Ashley twists 'input' into 'inputting' to mean implementing her choices
jokes.explanation: Clever wordplay showing Ashley's manipulative logic, though the setup is a bit drawn out

jokes.text: And I just changed my mind, you'll have no input whatsoever. / This is gonna be so much fun, us living together.
jokes.setup: Hilary takes back Ashley's input privileges
jokes.punchline: Ashley's sarcastic 'this is gonna be so much fun' about their living situation
jokes.explanation: Good ironic delivery showing the sisters' dynamic already deteriorating before they move in together

jokes.text: I'm gonna miss this time we've spend together. Me working hard, focusing on my career... and you sitting around in the house all day in your robe playing Nintendo.
jokes.setup: Carlton expresses sentiment about missing Will
jokes.punchline: Describes their dynamic as him working while Will plays Nintendo in a robe
jokes.explanation: Specific visual of Will's lazy lifestyle that rings true to his character - the Nintendo detail dates it but adds specificity

jokes.text: You always make me feel so, so superior.
jokes.setup: Carlton continues his comparison with Will
jokes.punchline: Thanks Will for making him feel superior
jokes.explanation: Classic Carlton arrogance disguised as gratitude - perfectly in character but predictable

jokes.text: Like the story of the grasshopper and the ant. The ant worked all summer long storing up food... while the grasshopper frittered away his time. Well, the winter has come and you've just been frittering. Frittering. Frittering. Frittering.
jokes.setup: Carlton tells the fable of the ant and grasshopper
jokes.punchline: Gets carried away repeating 'frittering' multiple times
jokes.explanation: Carlton getting caught up in his own moral superiority - the repetitive 'frittering' shows him savoring the judgment

jokes.text: You know the ladybug from the bookstore, the one the ant has the crush on? She even came over to spend some time with that fine grasshopper. The weekend he was away at Princeton.
jokes.setup: Will continues the fable metaphor
jokes.punchline: Reveals he hooked up with Carlton's crush while Carlton was away
jokes.explanation: Perfect escalation - not only reframes the fable but reveals he stole Carlton's love interest, turning the moral tale into a burn

jokes.text: Well, what was she doing here? / Frittering. / Mm. She fritters so good.
jokes.setup: Carlton asks what his crush was doing at Will's
jokes.punchline: Will uses Carlton's own word 'frittering' to imply sexual activity
jokes.explanation: Brilliant callback - takes Carlton's pretentious word and turns it into innuendo, completely subverting the moral lesson. Perfect structure and timing.

jokes.text: And soon enough, you're gonna be the only guy in the pool house. With the rest of us striking out on our own... the only question is, who's gonna move out first, you or Nicky?
jokes.setup: Carlton points out Will will be alone in the pool house
jokes.punchline: Suggests Will might move out after the 5-year-old
jokes.explanation: Good dig at Will's arrested development by comparing his timeline to a toddler's - hits the character dynamic well

jokes.text: Better hope he doesn't go to boarding school.
jokes.setup: The comparison between Will and Nicky moving out
jokes.punchline: Implies even a 5-year-old going to boarding school would leave Will behind
jokes.explanation: Good follow-up escalation - pushes the joke further by making Will's situation even more pathetic

jokes.text: My friend doesn't know what pensive means.
jokes.setup: Geoffrey asks if Will's 'friend' is feeling pensive
jokes.punchline: Will admits his friend doesn't know what pensive means
jokes.explanation: Self-aware humor about Will's education gaps while maintaining the thin 'friend' pretense - honest character moment

jokes.text: It's the tale of the young Earl of Monroe... who wanted to build a bridge over the River Mersey. He labored for years and years and finally he was all done and quite proud of himself.
jokes.setup: Geoffrey tells an inspirational story about persistence
jokes.punchline: Sets up what sounds like a success story
jokes.explanation: Good setup for the dark turn - establishes the expectation of an uplifting parable

jokes.text: Well, actually, when the earl completed the bridge, he flung himself off it. / Dude killed himself? / Quite.
jokes.setup: Will interprets the story as inspirational
jokes.punchline: Geoffrey reveals the earl committed suicide, confirms with 'Quite'
jokes.explanation: Masterful dark comedy - completely subverts expectation of inspiration with suicide, Geoffrey's casual 'Quite' is perfect deadpan delivery

jokes.text: That's the advice that your father gave you? / My father was not a well man.
jokes.setup: Will questions the appropriateness of the suicidal earl story
jokes.punchline: Geoffrey's massive understatement about his father's mental health
jokes.explanation: Perfect deadpan follow-up - 'not a well man' is hilariously inadequate for someone who tells suicide stories as inspiration

jokes.text: Yeah, I guess I could start by washing your pants in a thimble.
jokes.setup: Phil suggests Will could take Geoffrey's job
jokes.punchline: Will references washing Phil's enormous pants in a tiny thimble
jokes.explanation: Great visual joke about Phil's size - the absurd image of washing huge pants in a thimble is both specific and funny

jokes.text: Oh, not to worry, Big Guy. Will's gonna be sitting around this table for years to come.
jokes.setup: Phil expresses sentiment about missing family dinners
jokes.punchline: Carlton points out Will will still be there, calling Phil 'Big Guy'
jokes.explanation: Decent jab at Will's stagnation, though the 'Big Guy' feels like an unnecessary fat joke add-on

jokes.text: It's either that or the Homeboy Homeless Shelter. Ha!
jokes.setup: Carlton's comment about Will staying
jokes.punchline: Suggests Will's only alternative is the 'Homeboy Homeless Shelter' with a self-satisfied laugh
jokes.explanation: The 'Homeboy' wordplay is decent but Carlton laughing at his own joke undercuts the delivery

jokes.text: For your information, midget boy, I already got an apartment.
jokes.setup: Carlton's mockery about Will being stuck
jokes.punchline: Will's defensive lie about having an apartment, calling Carlton 'midget boy'
jokes.explanation: Sets up the episode's central lie - the height joke is predictable but Will's defensive reaction feels authentic

jokes.text: Don't toy with my emotions, Will. I've been waiting a long time, I'm very vulnerable.
jokes.setup: Carlton's excited reaction to Will moving out
jokes.punchline: Admits he's been waiting for this and is emotionally vulnerable about it
jokes.explanation: Great character moment - Carlton's desperate excitement about Will leaving is both funny and pathetic

jokes.text: The pool house is mine. Yes.
jokes.setup: Will confirms he's moving out
jokes.punchline: Carlton's triumphant claim to the pool house
jokes.explanation: Simple victory moment for Carlton - his immediate claiming of Will's space is perfectly in character

jokes.text: Looks like you've painted yourself into a corner this time, Master William.
jokes.setup: Geoffrey catches Will looking at apartment listings
jokes.punchline: Uses the idiom about painting into a corner while Will literally has paint swatches
jokes.explanation: Solid wordplay connecting the literal paint swatches to the metaphorical situation - Geoffrey's dry delivery helps

jokes.text: I'm one of the best corner-getter-outers that there is. You'll see.
jokes.setup: Geoffrey's corner metaphor
jokes.punchline: Will's made-up phrase 'corner-getter-outers'
jokes.explanation: Will's linguistic creativity shows his street smart vs formal education - made-up compound word has charm but limited impact

jokes.text: Within a week, I shall be at 30,000 feet headed to London... with many tiny bottles of Jim Beam dancing in front of me.
jokes.setup: Geoffrey explains he'll be leaving soon
jokes.punchline: Poetic description of getting drunk on airplane mini bottles
jokes.explanation: Geoffrey's poetic description of airline drinking is both elegant and revealing of his coping mechanisms

jokes.text: But until then... your back is mine.
jokes.setup: Geoffrey's departure timeline
jokes.punchline: Commits to helping Will until he leaves
jokes.explanation: Geoffrey's loyalty expressed in his characteristic formal style - 'your back is mine' is oddly elegant

jokes.text: When did the story fairy descend on this house?
jokes.setup: Phil wants to tell Will a story after Geoffrey's dark parable
jokes.punchline: Will notices everyone's been telling stories
jokes.explanation: Meta observation about the episode's structure - Will breaking the fourth wall by noticing the pattern of parables

jokes.text: Fine. Don't touch that channel! That cupcake's mine!
jokes.setup: Will visits an apartment
jokes.punchline: Landlord yells at invisible people about TV and food
jokes.explanation: Establishes the landlord's erratic behavior - creates uncomfortable apartment hunting scenario

jokes.text: I just thought somebody was there, you know, because... / No. I'm alone.
jokes.setup: Will hears the landlord yelling
jokes.punchline: Landlord insists he's alone despite clearly talking to someone
jokes.explanation: Uncomfortable comedy from mental illness - walks the line of taste but creates genuine awkwardness

jokes.text: Did you ever see that movie One Flew Over the Cuckoo's Nest?
jokes.setup: Landlord's erratic behavior
jokes.punchline: Will references the famous mental hospital movie
jokes.explanation: Direct reference connects the dots for audience - Will's film knowledge helps him process the bizarre situation

jokes.text: In lieu of my spring bonus, I think it would be a magnificent gesture... if you sent me back to London first class on the Concorde. / What would you consider an adequate gesture? / Business class with a kosher meal.
jokes.setup: Geoffrey negotiates his departure perks with Phil
jokes.punchline: Immediately lowers his expectations when challenged
jokes.explanation: Good negotiation comedy - Geoffrey's immediate downgrade from luxury to modest shows his practical nature

jokes.text: What would be less than adequate? / Working here another year.
jokes.setup: Phil continues the negotiation game
jokes.punchline: Geoffrey implies staying would be worse than no travel perks
jokes.explanation: Perfect escalation - Geoffrey's deadpan delivery of this brutal assessment of his job satisfaction is comedy gold

jokes.text: Will did it. / No one did anything. Now sit down.
jokes.setup: Phil calls for a family meeting
jokes.punchline: Carlton immediately blames Will for something that hasn't been mentioned
jokes.explanation: Classic sibling dynamic - Carlton's reflexive blame of Will before knowing what's wrong is perfectly petty

jokes.text: Then what's the point of moving? / Can I have that one back?
jokes.setup: Phil says the same rules apply after moving out
jokes.punchline: Ashley questions the point, Phil wants to retract the rule
jokes.explanation: Good parent-child dynamic - Phil's immediate regret about giving too much freedom is relatable

jokes.text: Hilary, you're 27. / That doesn't leave this room.
jokes.setup: Carlton claims to be 25, Hilary agrees
jokes.punchline: Phil corrects her age to 27, she demands secrecy
jokes.explanation: Classic Hilary vanity about her age - the immediate demand for confidentiality is perfectly in character

jokes.text: Oh, great. Now I can kiss Glee Club goodbye.
jokes.setup: Phil assigns Carlton to check on his sisters
jokes.punchline: Carlton sarcastically mourns losing Glee Club time
jokes.explanation: Perfect Carlton activity reference - Glee Club is exactly what he'd prioritize, delivered with genuine disappointment

jokes.text: Oh, no, Dad. November's sweeps month. No can do.
jokes.setup: Phil wants family together for Thanksgiving
jokes.punchline: Hilary prioritizes TV ratings period over family
jokes.explanation: Perfectly Hilary - prioritizing her shallow TV career over family traditions with casual dismissal

jokes.text: What if I meet somebody and they invite me to their house? / Well, what if I meet somebody? / Well, what if she meets somebody?
jokes.setup: Ashley questions Christmas plans if she meets someone
jokes.punchline: Carlton and Hilary pile on with their own hypothetical relationships
jokes.explanation: Good sibling escalation but the repetitive structure becomes predictable - works for character dynamics though

jokes.text: Man, this is less than what I'm paying now, Mr. Clark. / No, call me Peter, huh? / Oh, cool. Peter. / Hey, so when you moving out? / I'm not moving out.
jokes.setup: Will likes the apartment and price, landlord is friendly
jokes.punchline: Landlord reveals he's not actually moving out
jokes.explanation: Good bait-and-switch - builds up Will's excitement then pulls the rug out with the landlord's casual revelation

jokes.text: That's plenty of time to paint. Chop-chop.
jokes.setup: Geoffrey says he only has days left before leaving
jokes.punchline: 5-year-old Nicky dismisses this as plenty of time to paint the pool house
jokes.explanation: Brilliant - the toddler using Carlton's exact 'chop-chop' phrase shows learned entitled behavior, making the family dynamic generational

jokes.text: The word 'duh' comes to mind.
jokes.setup: Will asks what Geoffrey would say if Will admitted making a mistake
jokes.punchline: Geoffrey's understated but cutting response
jokes.explanation: Perfect Geoffrey - formal speech pattern delivering casual slang creates comedy through contrast

jokes.text: Uncle Phil can relate to that. He a man, he lumpy.
jokes.setup: Will talks about taking his lumps like a man
jokes.punchline: Connects Phil's manhood to his physical lumpiness
jokes.explanation: Clever wordplay connecting 'man's lumps' to Phil's physical appearance - Will's grammar adds authenticity

jokes.text: Dad, we've already been through this. We'll give you Columbus Day.
jokes.setup: Phil announces another family discussion about gatherings
jokes.punchline: Carlton offers the minor holiday as compromise
jokes.explanation: Good compromise humor - Columbus Day as the throwaway holiday they'll 'give' Phil shows their priorities

jokes.text: What are you, crazy? I mean that in the most respectful way.
jokes.setup: Phil announces selling the house
jokes.punchline: Will calls him crazy then immediately tries to soften it
jokes.explanation: Classic Will diplomacy failure - his attempt to soften the insult actually makes it funnier

jokes.text: I know what this is. I did an entire show on it. 'The change of life.' Are you experiencing mood swings? Trouble sleeping? Do your breasts ache?
jokes.setup: Phil announces house sale
jokes.punchline: Hilary diagnoses Phil with menopause symptoms
jokes.explanation: Hilary at her most oblivious - applying her shallow TV knowledge inappropriately, asking Phil about breast pain is peak comedy

jokes.text: I am not going through menopause. / Mood swing at 3 o'clock.
jokes.setup: Vivian denies menopause
jokes.punchline: Hilary immediately diagnoses the denial as a mood swing
jokes.explanation: Perfect follow-up - Hilary's commitment to her diagnosis even when confronted, treating normal responses as symptoms

jokes.text: Oh, yeah. It's soft, Uncle Phil. Marshmallowy.
jokes.setup: Carlton mentions soft market for house sales
jokes.punchline: Will agrees with increasingly absurd softness descriptors
jokes.explanation: Will's escalation from 'soft' to 'marshmallowy' shows his tendency to embellish - mild but consistent with character

jokes.text: Then let's dump this puppy.
jokes.setup: Phil says he'll make money on the house sale
jokes.punchline: Carlton immediately shifts to supporting the sale
jokes.explanation: Carlton's mercenary flip shows his priorities - money trumps sentiment, 'dump this puppy' is casual about the family home

jokes.text: Yeah, she grew up in this house, and Carlton still has a lot of growing to do.
jokes.setup: Ashley expresses attachment to the house where she grew up
jokes.punchline: Will points out Carlton still needs to grow (physically)
jokes.explanation: Standard height joke about Carlton - predictable but fits the character dynamic and moment

jokes.text: Did I say that?
jokes.setup: Everyone agrees to live together to avoid separation
jokes.punchline: Carlton realizes he inadvertently suggested it
jokes.explanation: Carlton's horror at accidentally suggesting they all live together - his regret is immediate and palpable

jokes.text: Oh. Um... I bought a monkey.
jokes.setup: Family asks about Will's big announcement after the house discussion
jokes.punchline: Will lies with the ridiculous claim he bought a monkey
jokes.explanation: Perfect absurdist ending - Will's panic lie is so random it's brilliant, 'I bought a monkey' is quotable and unexpected

tmdb_overview: The mansion's for sale and everybody's in the fast lane... except Will, who's stuck on life's soft shoulder.
